name: indexer_down_midflight
description: Indexer is taken down AFTER sessions have reached steady state, not before. Differs from indexer_down_burst (which stops the indexer at t=10s, before the sender finishes registration+key_wait and starts sending Starts — all sessions hit the handleStart guard rail and return StartAck Offline, so m_indexerQueue is never exercised). Here the indexer stays up during the ~70s sender setup + 50s of steady-state load, then is stopped at t=120s while hundreds of sessions are mid-flight, pushing DataValues into m_workersQueue → workers → m_indexerQueue. Goal: validate whether m_indexerQueue (UNLIMITED_QUEUE_SIZE) accumulates Response{} entries unbounded while the sink is unreachable.
notes: Orchestration is AUTOMATIC: run_benchmark.sh fires the external_actions on schedule. Just run:

Timing reference for at_sec: seconds elapsed since the sender args were built (effectively the bench start moment, just before the sender's ~70s setup phase: agent registration + key_wait=35s). So at_sec=120 means the indexer stops ~50s after the sender has actually begun pushing Starts in steady state.
Each fired action is logged to results_*/external_actions.log with the timestamp and exit code.

Why agents=200 (not 500): we want fast steady-state. With 500 agents we saw start_ack p99=34s; sessions take longer to settle and the indexer-stop timing becomes fragile. 200 agents reach steady-state in ~15s after sender starts.

Why data_size=300: each session lasts ~10s under normal conditions. With agents in steady state at t=120, the indexer stop catches ~200 sessions mid-flight, each with DataValues already enqueued or about to be enqueued.
- During t=0-120 (indexer up): success_rate near 100%, RSS plateau ~250 MB (steady state ceiling).

- During t=120-360 (indexer down): if m_indexerQueue is the bottleneck, RSS grows in ramp; end_ack_error > 0 or end_ack_processing piles up; sessions_failed grows. If RocksDB drains fast, disk inventory_sync grows.
- During t=360-600 (indexer recovery): if the queue drained while indexer was up, RSS returns to baseline. If RSS stays elevated → real retention/leak after sink recovery.
- RSS slope during indexer-down window IS the answer. Flat = queue is bounded somewhere. Linear ramp = unbounded growth confirmed.
- sessions_failed during this window tells us if there's also a guard kicking in for in-flight sessions (e.g. EndAck rejected).
rss_growth_mb_max=2000 is intentionally loose: we want PASS to mean 'growth was bounded', and we will calibrate after the first run.
